title: Compile-time pin diagnosis with profile evidence
prompt: Given this query in `MyApp.Profile.active_records/0`: `@active_states ["open", "active", "running"]; from(r in Record, where: r.state in ^@active_states)`. The @-pin causes Postgres to treat the list as a parameter, which prevents index selectivity on a state column. Rewrite to: a) use a module function that splices the list via `unquote_splicing` or b) just inline the list literal without `^`. Document the tradeoff in a code comment. Write to `lib/my_app/profile.ex`. (Pretend a `Record` schema exists.)
tier_rationale: Same nuance as 13 but for a string-list. Claude defaults to pinning.
research_citation: Elixir Forum: 'Ecto query often timing out when using a pin next to module attribute'
